paper_title: Archaeological and Parasitological Analysis at Epullán Chica

methods.description: This method involves examining coprolites collected from the archaeological site for the presence of parasites. The process includes rehydration, homogenization, sedimentation, and microscopic examination to identify parasites.

steps.description: The coprolites were described, measured and weighted.
The smaller samples were whole processed by rehydration in a 0.5% water solution of trissodium phosphate (TSP) in a glass tube for at least 72 h.
steps.searchable_summary: rehydrate small coprolite samples in TSP solution

steps.description: Followed by homogenization, filtered and processed by spontaneous sedimentation.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize, filter, and sediment coprolite samples

steps.description: In the case of bigger samples, 0.5 g from the surface and the interior of coprolites was rehydrated.
steps.searchable_summary: rehydrate 0.5g from surface and interior of large coprolite samples

steps.description: Twenty slides of each sample were made with the aid of a drop of sediment mixed with one drop of glycerin and examined at 100× and 400× using a light microscopy.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides and examine coprolite samples under microscope

steps.description: Egg dimensions and morphologies were compared with data from the literature in order to identify the parasites at the lowest taxonomic level.
steps.searchable_summary: compare egg dimensions and morphologies with literature

methods.description: This method involves separating and analyzing macroscopic remains from coprolites to study the diet of past populations.

steps.description: The macroscopic remains were separated and dried at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: separate and dry macroscopic remains from coprolites

steps.description: The remains were examined for diet analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: examine macroscopic remains for diet analysis
